title: React Lifting State Up Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of sharing state between components and the single source of truth principle in React.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the best strategy when two sibling components need to access and modify the same state in React?
options: Duplicate the state in both components.
Lift the shared state up to their closest common ancestor.
Use a global variable.
Directly access each other's state.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of lifting state up?
options: It makes the app run faster.
It ensures that multiple components always reflect the same shared data and stay in sync.
It hides data from other components.
It reduces the number of components.

questions.question: According to the article, what should you pass down from the parent to child components along with the shared state?
options: Only the current state value.
Any necessary state updater functions as callbacks.
A copy of the whole parent component.
A reference to the window object.

questions.question: In React, should we avoid having multiple 'sources of truth' for the same data?
options: Yes, always.
No, it's better to have multiple copies for safety.
Yes, but only in large apps.
Only if you're using class components.

questions.question: Is lifting state up the only way to share data in React (alternatives exist like Context API etc.)?
options: Yes, always.
No, but it's a very common and recommended pattern for simple data sharing.
Yes, but only in functional components.
None of the above.
